fix_pr_title: fix(VCST-4987): rollback text section maxLength validation for configurable products
str_result: 0/3 end-to-end; 3/3 on client-side step 5 (no error, button enabled); 3/3 fail at step 6 (Add-to-cart silent no-op — server rejects customText='TEST_QA' with CONFIGURATION_SECTION_CUSTOM_TEXT_MAX_LENGTH_EXCEEDED)
regression_issues: Server-side asymmetry: AddItem rejects customText='TEST_QA' (maxLength=5) but ChangeCartConfiguredItem accepts it — user can persist a configuration value via edit that Add refuses to create
Silent Add-to-cart failure: server returns HTTP 200 with validationErrors[] but UI does not surface the error; button resets and nothing visible changes — worse UX than original bug
PR rolled back client-side maxLength entirely instead of scoping to Custom-input only (presets should bypass maxLength AND be serialized as optionId reference, not customText)
side_effects: Secondary defect (Custom textbox not cleared on Custom → preset switch): still present, out of this PR's scope
business_rules_verified: Required section blocks Add-to-cart when empty (PASS)
BL-CART-*: configured item adds to cart and persists (verified only via Custom input 'abc' path — NOT via TEST_QA preset, which never enters the cart)
business_rules_violated: Intended contract (per user clarification): section.maxLength should apply only to Custom input, not to preset option labels — current fix violates this by disabling client validation entirely
recommendation: Reopen. Rescope the fix: (1) keep client-side maxLength for Custom-input path only, (2) serialize preset selections by optionId reference instead of copying label into customText, (3) surface server validationErrors in UI, (4) harmonize AddItem/ChangeCartConfiguredItem server validation.
